channel.title: The Athletic - Ohio State Buckeyes (College Football)
channel.description: Ohio State Buckeyes college football news from The Athletic (NYTimes).

item.title: What we heard at Big Ten meetings: No push to mandate 10 Power 4 opponents amid CFP talks
item.description: Every SEC, ACC and Big 12 team will play 10 Power 4 opponents in 2026, but four Big Ten teams will only play nine.

item.title: What I expect from Ohio State's roster: 44 thoughts on 44 defensive players (and kickers)
item.description: Matt Patricia has seven NFL Draft picks to replace, but Ohio State's defense is loaded with talent again.

item.title: Michigan-Ohio State rivalry is enough reason to bag a 24-team College Football Playoff
item.description: Congrats, Big Ten commissioner Tony Petitti, you’ve found a way to make your most valuable piece of inventory as worthless as possible.

item.title: Who would have made a 24-team field in the College Football Playoff era? Try 80 different teams
item.description: Eighty teams would have made a 24-team CFP over the last 12 seasons. Here's what we learned from mocking 12 years of brackets.

item.title: Recruiting Report: 'Scare tactics,' QB carousel, USC's follow-up, Texas A&M's tear
item.description: Texas A&M's 2027 class already includes four five-star prospects, including the nation's top offensive lineman.

item.title: Big Ten, Tony Petitti all in on 24-team CFP: 'We feel strongly about it'
item.description: "Inside our league," the Big Ten commissioner said, "there is a deep commitment to 24 and the access."

item.title: What you need to know about Ohio State's 2026 roster: 44 thoughts on 44 offensive players
item.description: Ohio State has big expectations coming into this season. Here's a position-by-position breakdown of the Buckeyes' offensive roster.

item.title: What are your thoughts on a 24-team College Football Playoff? Let us know
item.description: Do you want the Playoff to expand? Are you attached to conference championship games? Let us know in this survey.

item.title: Ryan Day: Eliminating Big Ten title game would enhance Ohio State-Michigan showdown
item.description: Michigan leads the all-time series 62-52-6 going back to 1897.

item.title: Revisiting the top 10 college football recruiting classes of the 2023 cycle
item.description: Nick Saban’s next-to-last recruiting class included two five-stars who turned into first-round picks in three years.

item.title: Meet Tennessee commit Derrick Baker, the most under-ranked QB prospect in the '27 class
item.description: Baker maintains his commitment to the Vols is strong, but other programs — including Georgia — are pursuing the talented signal caller.

item.title: College football transfer portal DB rankings: Koi Perich, Earl Little Jr. top list
item.description: Seven of the top 20 defensive back transfers, including the top four, will play in the Big Ten in 2026.

item.title: Submit a question for Stewart Mandel's College Football Mailbag
item.description: Life got you down? Ticked about the 76-team NCAA tournament? Yeah, me too. So let’s talk college football instead. Leave your questions here. Please include your city.

item.title: Which Big Ten teams can win it all in the next five years? Here's what fans think
item.description: Big Ten fans may talk a big game, but when pressed on their teams' College Football Playoff outlook, self-awareness takes over.

item.title: Ohio State has the ‘best linebackers coach in the country,’ and he's just getting started
item.description: James Laurinaitis could become Ohio State's next Brian Hartline, and he's only two years in.

item.title: How do Big Ten football fans feel right now? Where readers stand on Petitti, CFP and more
item.description: A survey of more than 3,500 readers shows where public opinion around the Big Ten lies as the league builds off a CFP three-peat.

item.title: Submit a question for Stewart Mandel's College Football Mailbag
item.description: The Brendan Sorsby saga continues to dominate the news, but there’s plenty more college football to discuss. Leave your questions here. Please include your city.

item.title: Big Ten schools receive record $1.37 billion from 2024-25 fiscal year
item.description: The 2024-25 fiscal year was the Big Ten’s first with 18 members and coincided with a new media rights deal and the new 12-team CFP.

item.title: Is QB Julian Sayin a first-round talent? Was OL coach Tyler Bowen a bad hire? Ohio State Mailbag
item.description: Cameron Teague Robinson digs into the Buckeyes football mailbag, including a question about quarterback Julian Sayin's NFL Draft prospects.

item.title: College football transfer portal DL rankings: Damon Wilson II, Princewill Umanmielen top list
item.description: Seven of the top 20 D-line transfers are headed to the SEC, six to the Big 12, four to the Big Ten, two to Notre Dame and one to the ACC.

item.title: Brendan Sorsby and the transfer portal's vetting problem; is the 2027 QB class overhyped? Mandel's Mailbag
item.description: With how fast the transfer portal moves, how much can schools really find out about players they're paying millions?

item.title: Submit a question for Stewart Mandel's College Football Mailbag
item.description: There was some big news out of Texas Tech on Monday, and this week’s Mailbag is going to run a day later than usual on Thursday. What else might come out? Leave your questions here. Please include your city.

item.title: Submit your Ohio State football questions for Cameron Teague Robinson’s mailbag
item.description: The spring game is over. The roster is set. What’s on your mind? Submit your Ohio State football questions below.

item.title: How prepared is Ohio State to replace another 11 NFL Draft picks? What we've learned so far
item.description: The Buckeyes have led all colleges in NFL Draft picks each of the past two years. Here's how their 2026 roster is coming together.

item.title: Buffalo Bills draft Ohio State cornerback Davison Igbinosun with 62nd pick in 2026 NFL Draft
item.description: Igbinosun was an outside boundary cornerback and three-year starter for the Buckeyes.
